description: Phonological accuracy evaluation protocol for NepTTS-Bench. The core differentiator for Nepali TTS — scores whether the system correctly produces Nepali phonological contrasts that distinguish meaning. Covers aspiration, retroflexion, nasalization, gemination, and schwa deletion.

notes.usage: Run ABX discrimination and forced alignment on all phonological minimal pair utterances. Generate the perceptual confusion matrix. Supplement with human evaluation on a 50-pair subset. This is the most Nepali-specific evaluation dimension.
notes.evaluation: A system that fails phonological accuracy cannot be considered acceptable for Nepali TTS, regardless of acoustic quality scores. Phonological contrasts are meaning-distinguishing — confusing aspirated/unaspirated or retroflex/dental produces a different word.
notes.reference: Contrast categories derived from ../data/phonological_minimal_pairs.json. Thresholds calibrated against human perception studies of Nepali phonological contrasts.

test_data.description: Minimal pairs covering Nepali phonological contrasts. Each pair consists of two words/utterances differing in exactly one phonological feature.

abx_discrimination.description: ABX discrimination test — given three audio samples (A, B, X) where A and B are a minimal pair and X matches one of them, can a classifier correctly identify which one X matches? Measures whether the TTS system produces phonologically distinct outputs for contrastive sounds.
abx_discrimination.method: Extract speaker-normalized embeddings (ECAPA-TDNN or wav2vec2) from synthesized minimal pair audio. For each pair, compute cosine distance between A-X and B-X. Classify X as matching the closer embedding. Score = percentage of correct classifications.

aspiration_4way.description: 4-way aspiration contrasts across 5 places of articulation (velar, palatal, retroflex, dental, bilabial): voiceless unaspirated, voiceless aspirated, voiced unaspirated, voiced aspirated

schwa_deletion.description: Correct schwa deletion vs retention in context — Nepali deletes inherent schwa in specific environments

forced_alignment.description: Forced alignment of generated audio to the expected phoneme sequence. Measures per-phoneme confidence and identifies systematic alignment failures.
forced_alignment.pronunciation_dictionary: Nepali grapheme-to-phoneme mapping covering all test pairs

alignment_confidence.description: Per-phoneme posterior probability from forced alignment. Low confidence indicates the produced sound doesn't match the expected phoneme.

boundary_precision.description: Precision of phoneme boundary placement compared to expected durations.

perceptual_confusion_matrix.description: Aggregated confusion matrix showing which Nepali phonological contrasts the TTS system fails to produce distinctly. Built from ABX errors and forced alignment failures.
key_contrasts_to_monitor: Aspiration: voiceless unaspirated ↔ voiceless aspirated (e.g., क↔ख)
Aspiration: voiced unaspirated ↔ voiced aspirated (e.g., ग↔घ)
perceptual_confusion_matrix.reporting: Report top-10 most confused pairs with error counts and example utterances

human_evaluation.description: Human ABX listening test on a subset of phonological minimal pairs to validate automated metrics and establish perceptual ground truth.
human_evaluation.sampling: 50 minimal pairs stratified across all contrast categories, prioritizing pairs where automated metrics show borderline scores
human_evaluation.listener_requirements: Native Nepali speakers with self-reported normal hearing, familiar with standard Nepali pronunciation
human_evaluation.task: ABX format — listener hears three audio clips (A from word1, B from word2, X from one of them) and identifies which word X matches

procedure: Generate audio for all minimal pairs from ../data/phonological_minimal_pairs.json
Run ABX discrimination using ECAPA-TDNN and wav2vec2 embeddings
Run Montreal Forced Aligner on all generated audio with expected phoneme sequences
Compute per-category ABX accuracy and forced alignment confidence
Generate perceptual confusion matrix from all errors
Select 50-pair subset for human evaluation (stratified, prioritizing borderline cases)
Conduct human ABX listening test with 3 native speakers
Compare automated vs human ABX scores to validate automated metrics
Run identical pipeline on teacher (Gemini) output for baseline comparison
Report per-category pass/fail with confusion analysis

aggregate_reporting.description: How to report phonological accuracy results.
aggregate_reporting.pass_rate: Percentage of minimal pairs meeting 'pass' threshold per category
aggregate_reporting.minimum_rate: Percentage of minimal pairs meeting 'minimum' threshold per category
visualizations: Per-category ABX accuracy bar chart (with pass/minimum threshold lines)
Forced alignment confidence distribution per category
Human vs automated ABX correlation scatter plot
